subject: Public Health
title: Drug Overdose Prevention
description: A quick, practical guide to Drug Overdose Prevention.

hook.text: Overdose is now the leading cause of death for Americans under 50—but most overdose deaths are preventable.

buildup.text: Opioid overdose slows breathing to a stop—death occurs within minutes without intervention.

discovery.text: Naloxone—sprayed in the nose or injected—reverses opioid overdose within minutes and has no abuse potential.

twist.text: Supervised consumption sites—where people use drugs under medical watch—have prevented thousands of deaths with zero.

climax.text: Fentanyl test strips, naloxone distribution, and Good Samaritan laws all reduce overdose deaths—when implemented.

punchline.text: People don't have to die from overdose. We have the tools. We need the will.

quiz.question: How does naloxone work?
options: It reverses opioid overdose within minutes
